title: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse
display_title: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse
wikitext:
[]: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Mon Jan 14, 2019 4:54 pm So I'm new here! Been talking about Eva over on Youtube for half a year or so and decided to start using Evageeks recently. Well anyway here's a fun little discussion I thought of. One night you go to sleep and the next thing you know you and your family are in the Eva Verse on the day of the Third Angels attack. You quickly realize that just down the road is Shinji Ikari on the phone waiting for Misato Katsuragi. Now with Shinji in the Eva Verse, it's up to you. Do not involve yourself or do you try to stop Gendo and Seele and help the pilots? Do you tell your family what you know and try to figure out what's going on or do nothing? Edit: As pointed out you can't do anything if you don't speak the language. So as of now you and your family can now speak Japanese if you could not before!

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Jan 15, 2019 1:15 pm In that scenario, I doubt very much being able to affect the outcome of the story, even with knowledge of the outcome. The way I would look at it is that I would then be stranded in Japan, and I don't know the language, and I have no means to provide for myself, I would need to get back to the US, and then do whatever the hell I want (for the most part) until the world inevitably (for all intents and purposes) ends. Live it up, so to speak. Or, maybe I can go to the Nerv branch at MIT and say "I know this is going to sound crazy... but...".

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Tue Jan 15, 2019 1:19 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: II don't know the language, .".

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Tue Jan 15, 2019 1:24 pm Try enjoy the time I have of life before shit hits the fan.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Tue Jan 15, 2019 1:50 pm Ambiant23 wrote: Try enjoy the time I have of life before shit hits the fan.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Chuckman » Tue Jan 15, 2019 1:57 pm Literally anything you say anywhere in the world to anything affiliated with NERV will end with you committing suicide via two shots to the back of the head. I'd spend the remaining roughly nine months of my life in intense meditation in hopes that when the apotheosis comes I will transcend death and consume the entire human race until there is nothing left but me. If that doesn't work it's not like I have anything better to do.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Tue Jan 15, 2019 2:30 pm Chuckman wrote: Literally anything you say anywhere in the world to anything affiliated with NERV will end with you committing suicide via two shots to the back of the head. I'd spend the remaining roughly nine months of my in intense meditation in hopes that when the apotheosis comes I will transcend death and consume the entire human race until there is nothing left but me. If that doesn't work it's not like I have anything better to do.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Joseki » Tue Jan 15, 2019 5:11 pm I try to fuck Misato then wait to go "pop" in a sea of souls. More realistically I'll get stomped on by Sachiel I think.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Tue Jan 15, 2019 6:05 pm Joseki wrote: I try to fuck Misato then wait to go "pop" in a sea of souls. More realistically I'll get stomped on by Sachiel I think.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Wed Jan 16, 2019 1:51 pm Joseki wrote: I try to fuck Misato.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Jan 16, 2019 3:45 pm I might be a bit cynical, but a) I am living in Germany, and Germany is still a real country in Eva I see no reason to be transported to any other location other than the one I am in right now, so I probably wouldn't experience a lot of the action (or even know about anything, given how highly secretive the whole thing is... although that's up for debate) and b) third impact/NTI canonically occurs at the end of 2015/start of 2016 and we are living in 2019, I would wake up in post-impact hell land

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Jan 16, 2019 6:33 pm I didn't think of that. That changes things. I appear exactly where I am in 2016 in the Evaverse: Given how sea levels changed due to Third Impact, I will be dropped in the ocean miles from shore with only a minimal ability to swim, off the Mid-Atlantic coast in January. I'm dead in 3 minutes at most. I appear near the characters: None of them can understand or help me, it may only be two of them anyway, and if I can't scavenge several medications that I need and absolutely can't stop taking, I'll die in 1-2 weeks. So the answer is: Die. Hopefully I get no. 2 since I'll have options and I'm terrified of drowning. Seems like a bad way to go.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Melkor » Wed Jan 16, 2019 6:51 pm I guess a better question to ask would be: suppose you reformed from the sea of LCL (fully healed and healthy) in the post-Third Impact world. What do you do next?

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 17, 2019 7:50 am Probably die due to the lack of infrastructure.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Thu Jan 17, 2019 9:05 am Live a life of famine and poverty

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Thu Jan 17, 2019 7:46 pm Blockio wrote: I might be a bit cynical, but a) I am living in Germany, and Germany is still a real country in Eva I see no reason to be transported to any other location other than the one I am in right now, so I probably wouldn't experience a lot of the action (or even know about anything, given how highly secretive the whole thing is... although that's up for debate) and b) third impact/NTI canonically occurs at the end of 2015/start of 2016 and we are living in 2019, I would wake up in post-impact hell land

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Ambiant23 » Fri Jan 18, 2019 8:43 am Nerd King 101 wrote: You appear at the start of the series so I guess you could like track down and talk to Asuka and like befriend her and talk about her feelings or something.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Nerd King 101 » Sat Jan 19, 2019 12:20 pm Ambiant23 wrote: You do remember that they live in like a secret organization right?

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Melkor » Sat Jan 19, 2019 5:03 pm Would all the cities built by humans still be intact though? As far as I could tell, the only thing Human Instrumentality did was make all the humans turn into LCL. It didn't seem to destroy any of their homes or buildings, so if people did start to come back from the sea of LCL, it would be easy for them to just go back to living as they were before. As for food, would it be possible to obtain nourishment from the LCL, either by soaking in it or drinking it? Granted, there's the possibility you might be drinking someone, but if its the only option you have to survive while waiting for humanity to come back, I'll take it.

[]: Re: You Wake Up In The Eva Verse             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 20, 2019 1:38 pm Physical structures being intact doesn't offset the fact that the human machine that had kept everything moving was completely destroyed with 3I. What happens now? Things won't be back to the way they were for a long time, if ever. How do you live the way you did before unless all the utility workers are hard at work? Farmers? Designers, engineers, manufacturers? Shippers and distributors? Who upholds the law? Who decides the law? It's been established that people have to individually manifest the will to return to physical form, meaning infrastructure won't magically resurrect itself. It will need to be rebuilt incrementally. Those who come back have to pick up the pieces as best as they can, whatever their pre-3I specialization might be. If you're talking about the LCL in the ocean, it's mixed with sea water. Dehydration is a much more immediate risk than starvation, so you probably don't want to drink the stuff.

As for food, would it be possible to obtain nourishment from the LCL, either by soaking in it or drinking it? Dehydration is a much more immediate risk than starvation, so you probably don't want to drink the stuff.
